agent.description: The agent to use for the component

agentConfiguration.description: AgentConfiguration used by Camel to create the agent internally. When set, Camel creates an AgentWithMemory if a ChatMemoryProvider is configured, otherwise an AgentWithoutMemory. If an agentFactory is also configured, the factory takes precedence.

agentFactory.description: The agent factory to use for creating agents if no Agent is provided

jsonSchema.description: JSON schema for structured output validation. Only supported in inline agent creation mode: agentConfiguration must be set and neither agent nor agentFactory may be configured. Mutually exclusive with outputClass.

lazyStartProducer.label: producer
lazyStartProducer.description: Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.

outputClass.description: Java class to use for structured output. Camel derives the JSON schema from the class and instructs the model to produce matching JSON; the response body is left as a raw JSON string. Only supported in inline agent creation mode: agentConfiguration must be set and neither agent nor agentFactory may be configured. The class must be a POJO with public fields or getters; simple types, enums, and collections are not supported. Mutually exclusive with jsonSchema.

tags.description: Tags for discovering and calling Camel route tools

autowiredEnabled.label: advanced
autowiredEnabled.description: Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection factories, AWS Clients, etc.

mcpClients.label: advanced
mcpClients.description: Pre-built MCP (Model Context Protocol) client instances for external tool integration. Reference beans from the registry, e.g., #myMcpClient1,#myMcpClient2

mcpServer.label: advanced
mcpServer.description: MCP server definitions in the form of mcpServer..=. Supported properties: transportType (stdio, http, streamableHttp, or sse, default: stdio), command (comma-separated, for stdio), url (for http/sse), environment.= (for stdio), timeout (in seconds, default: 60), logRequests, logResponses, oauthProfile (OAuth profile for HTTP auth, requires camel-oauth). This is a multi-value option with prefix: mcpServer.

CamelLangChain4jAgentUserMessage.description: The user message to accompany file content when using WrappedFile as input.

CamelLangChain4jAgentMediaType.description: The media type (MIME type) of the file content. Overrides auto-detection from file extension.

CamelLangChain4jAgentExcludeTags.description: Comma-separated list of Camel tool tags to exclude from this agent invocation.

CamelLangChain4jAgentExcludeMcpServers.description: Comma-separated list of MCP server names (keys) to exclude from this agent invocation.
